title: Stage 4: Execution
subtitle: The selected warm path is translated into a draft, approval state, and Mission Control handoff.
summary: Execution should keep the chosen investor route visible instead of hiding the context behind a generic send button. The founder can still see who the message is for, why this is the right warm path, and where Mission Control takes over.
callouts: The founder stays in control with a visible approval gate before anything moves toward LinkedIn execution.
Mission Control is the execution bridge, not the ranking engine or the strategist.
For the hackathon build, this stage stops at a real payload handoff instead of auto-send.

connector.name: Harrison Dahme
connector.title: CTO @ Hack VC | Engineering & Ventures | Agent Framework & Blockchain Lab Co-Founder

likelyInvestorDestination.name: Hack VC venture path
likelyInvestorDestination.description: The final presentation route Tigerclaw is carrying into Mission Control.

narrative.warmPathSummary: Execution keeps the selected Harrison -> Hack VC path visible so the founder never loses sight of why this message exists and what it is trying to unlock.
narrative.whyThisPerson: The system commits to Harrison here because the direct route is still the strongest mix of venture proximity, warmth, and credibility for this presentation flow.
narrative.whyNow: By the time this stage is reached, the founder already has the right memo, traction snapshot, and relationship context assembled, so the path is ready for a controlled handoff.
narrative.whyThisRankedAboveOthers: This becomes the final demo route because it is both the most legible investor story and the easiest one to explain live: warm relationship, direct venture relevance, narrow ask, clear next step.
narrative.suggestedAsk: Approve the context-first Harrison draft and hand it to Mission Control as the downstream execution payload.
supportingProof: Recent conversation context is already captured.
The Hack VC fit memo and Tigerclaw traction snapshot are ready to attach.
The approval gate keeps the founder in control before any downstream action.
narrative.likelyObjection: The only remaining risk is sending before the founder is comfortable with the framing, which is why the approval gate stays visible here.
narrative.nextBestStep: Approve the draft, create the Mission Control payload, and keep the live send as a downstream manual step.
narrative.draftMessage: Hey Harrison, great chatting recently about AI-agent infrastructure and crypto-adjacent product strategy. I am building Tigerclaw, which helps founders turn their existing network into the warmest investor path, and I think there is a specific overlap with the kind of founder and infrastructure context Hack VC sees. I put together a very short note on why I think the fit is real, plus a concise traction snapshot. Would you be open to a quick calibration call, and if it resonates, point me toward the right next investor path rather than me guessing from the outside?

executionRows.label: Route mode
executionRows.value: Final chosen route for the founder-facing demo.

executionRows.label: Draft posture
executionRows.value: This message is ready to move through approval into a Mission Control payload.
